sections.heading: Overview
sections.content:
FX BRIEF: ASIAN SESSION ANALYSIS
11 June 2026 • 05:27 CET

HEADLINE: GLOBAL FX OVERVIEW — ASIAN SESSION

The dollar trades in neutral consolidation mode during Asian hours as markets digest recent hawkish Federal Reserve rhetoric while awaiting fresh directional catalysts. USD strength intraday registers -0.022, reflecting a balanced tape with no clear momentum bias. Risk sentiment remains cautiously constructive, though commodity currencies continue to struggle against persistent weakness from the prior session. USD/JPY's push above 160.50 keeps intervention risks elevated, while European majors exhibit modest resilience. The dominant theme remains divergent central bank expectations, with rate differential dynamics supporting the greenback's medium-term trajectory despite intraday consolidation. Asian equity markets trade mixed, offering little guidance for FX direc

sections.heading: Analysis
sections.content:
tional flows.

CURRENCY STRENGTH: INTRADAY RANKING

1. JPY: +0.15% — Safe-haven flows amid intervention speculation and technical resistance at 160.50
2. EUR: +0.16% — Modest rebound from oversold conditions; ECB policy divergence narratives easing
3. GBP: +0.10% — Sterling finds footing above 1.3350 support; data calendar light
4. USD: -0.02% — Neutral consolidation; no fresh catalysts driving directional conviction
5. CHF: -0.13% — Profit-taking after recent safe-haven rally; SNB policy outlook stable
6. CAD: -0.12% — Oil price stabilization insufficient to offset broader commodity currency weakness
7. AUD: -0.14% — China growth concerns weigh; iron ore weakness continues
8. NZD: -0.15% — Worst performer; dairy price softness and RBNZ dovish expectations pressuring kiwi

FX MAJORS — KEY PAIRS

EUR/USD: Trading 1.1554, up 0.16% intraday but down 0.50% on the settleme

sections.heading: Outlook
sections.content:
nt. The pair finds modest buying interest above 1.1520 support as short-covering provides temporary relief. Upside capped at 1.1580 resistance zone. Bias remains cautiously neutral-to-bearish on rate differential headwinds, but immediate downside momentum exhausted. Watch 1.1600 for any bullish continuation.

GBP/USD: Cable at 1.3386, gaining 0.10% intraday though down 0.30% on settlement. The pair consolidates above 1.3350 with light two-way flows dominating Asian trade. Resistance at 1.3420 remains intact. UK economic data calendar quiet this week, leaving sterling vulnerable to broader dollar dynamics. Bias neutral with slight bearish undertones below 1.3400.

USD/JPY: Trading 160.5010, up 0.07% intraday and 0.32% on settlement. The pair probes fresh multi-year highs, intensifying verbal intervention warnings from Japanese officials. Market expects MOF/BOJ action if
